tweets.text:
many poor practices work rn at the frontier of AI apps not because they’re tenable but because things are still fundamentally being shaped, and users will put up with bug-ridden AI software because the market has not settled yet

doesn’t mean poor practices are the future

seeing a lot of companies dealing with internal allocatation of token budgets with a lot more fierce competition between teams for token $$.

think there will be premiums on
- who can show ROI for tokens used
- teams and people's innate ability to use tokens well.

A lot of folks talk about "escaping the permanent underclass". If AGI pans out, the future class divide won't be based on wealth, but on cognitive agency. There will be a "focus class" (those who control their attention and actually do things) and a "slop class" (those whose
Collectively hundreds of millions of hours spent on this class of CSS problems, solved in a few weeks with AI agents.

Build the thing that feels too ambitious.

If you're using agents to do exactly what you were doing before - just faster - you may be thinking too small. 

Ambitious is the right size side-project.

breathe intelligence into everything that moves 🦾 

great work from Stanford on using π0 for drone control
